title: W3 B Continuity
body:
Mathematical Economics

Juan Carlos Carbajal

School of Economics UNSW‚ÄďSydney

Week 3B ‚ÄĒ Term 1, 2026

1/18   Existence of Solution to the UMP

Recall the consumer‚Äôs utility maximization problem. First order of business is to make sure it can be solved.

Proposition 1

Given a continuous utility function u : X ‚Üí R, the consumer‚Äôs utility maximization problem (UMP) max u(x) subject to p¬∑x ‚Č§ m x‚Č•0

has at least one solution.

Existence of solutions to optimal behavior problems relies (in part) on the continuity properties of functions.

2/18  Continuity of Real-Valued Functions Defined on R

3/18

Intuitively, f is continuous at a point xŐā if we can get arbitrarily close to the value f (xŐā) by getting sufficiently close to xŐā.

4/18

Definition 1

Let I ‚äÜ R be an open interval and f : I ‚Üí R. (a) f is continuous at xŐā ‚ąą I if for every ŌĶ > 0 there exists some őī > 0 such that for all x ‚ąą I, |x ‚ąí xŐā| < őī =‚áí |f (x) ‚ąí f (xŐā)| < ŌĶ.

Remark In the above definition, őī > 0 may depend on ŌĶ and on xŐā.

5/18

Definition 1

Let I ‚äÜ R be an open interval and f : I ‚Üí R. (a) f is continuous at xŐā ‚ąą I if for every ŌĶ > 0 there exists some őī > 0 such that for all x ‚ąą I, |x ‚ąí xŐā| < őī =‚áí |f (x) ‚ąí f (xŐā)| < ŌĶ.

(b) f is a continuous function if it is continuous at every point on its domain I.

Remark In the above definition, őī > 0 may depend on ŌĶ and on xŐā.

|f1 (x) ‚ąí f1 (xŐā)| = |őĪ x ‚ąí őĪ xŐā| = |őĪ| |x ‚ąí xŐā|

ŌĶ ‚ó¶ Let őī ‚ąą R be such that őī = |őĪ| > 0.

‚ó¶ If x ‚ąą R is such that |x ‚ąí xŐā| < őī, we obtain

|f1 (x) ‚ąí f1 (xŐā)| = |őĪ x ‚ąí őĪ xŐā| = |őĪ| |x ‚ąí xŐā| < |őĪ| őī = ŌĶ.

ŌĶ ‚ó¶ Let őī ‚ąą R be such that őī = |őĪ| > 0.

|f1 (x) ‚ąí f1 (xŐā)| = |őĪ x ‚ąí őĪ xŐā| = |őĪ| |x ‚ąí xŐā| < |őĪ| őī = ŌĶ.

‚ó¶ It follows that f1 is continuous at xŐā.

‚ó¶ Since xŐā was arbitrarily chosen, f1 is continuous on I1 = R.

Example 3 (c)

f3 : (‚ąí1, 1) ‚Üí R is defined by Ô£Ī Ô£≥1 for x > 0.

To show that f3 is continuous at any xŐā < 0, fix ŌĶ > 0. ‚ó¶ Choose őī = 21 |xŐā| > 0.

‚ó¶ For any x ‚ąą (‚ąí1, 1), if |x ‚ąí xŐā| < őī then we have

x ‚ąí xŐā ‚Č§ |x ‚ąí xŐā| < őī = ‚ąí 21 xŐā.

‚ó¶ This means that x < 12 xŐā < 0.

‚ó¶ Thus |f3 (x) ‚ąí f3 (xŐā)| = 0 < ŌĶ.

9/18   Example 3 (c)

f3 : (‚ąí1, 1) ‚Üí R is defined by Ô£Ī Ô£≥1 for x > 0.

To show that f3 is discontinuous at xŐā = 0, we are required to find some ŌĶ > 0 for which the definition fails. ‚ó¶ Choose ŌĶ = 12 > 0.

‚ó¶ For any őī > 0, the őī‚Äďinterval at xŐā = 0 includes strictly positive reals.

‚ó¶ Take any such real number: 0 < x < őī.

‚ó¶ Then |f3 (x) ‚ąí f3 (xŐā)| = |1 ‚ąí 0| = 1 > 21 = ŌĶ.

10/18   Application: Continuous Preferences

There is only one composite good in the economy. Anne has preferences represented by a continuous utility u : R ‚Üí R. ‚ó¶ Suppose Anne strictly prefers to consume x to y. Note we are not saying that x > y. Anne may not be greedy.

‚ó¶ Since her preferences are continuous, consuming slightly more or less than x should also be strictly preferred than consuming y.

Proposition 2

If Anne‚Äôs utility function u : R ‚Üí R is continuous and u(x) > u(y), then there exists some őī > 0 such that for all x‚Ä≤ ‚ąą (x ‚ąí őī, x + őī), one has u(x‚Ä≤ ) > u(y).

11/18   Application: Continuous Preferences

Before proving Proposition 2, recall that for any real number a and any ŌĶ > 0, |a| < ŌĶ if and only if ‚ąí ŌĶ < a < ŌĶ. (1)

12/18   Application: Continuous Preferences

Before proving Proposition 2, recall that for any real number a and any ŌĶ > 0, |a| < ŌĶ if and only if ‚ąí ŌĶ < a < ŌĶ. (1)

Proof Fix ŌĶ = u(x) ‚ąí u(y) > 0. ‚ó¶ Since u is continuous at x, there is some őī > 0 such that for all x‚Ä≤ ‚ąą (x ‚ąí őī, x + őī), one has |u(x‚Ä≤ ) ‚ąí u(x)| < ŌĶ.

‚ó¶ For any such x‚Ä≤ , using Equation (1) we can write

‚ąí ŌĶ < u(x‚Ä≤ ) ‚ąí u(x) < ŌĶ.

‚ó¶ Replacing ‚ąíŌĶ = u(y) ‚ąí u(x) above obtains

u(y) ‚ąí u(x) < u(x‚Ä≤ ) ‚ąí u(x).

‚ó¶ The result follows.

12/18  Intermediate Value Theorem

13/18   Continuity on a Closed Interval

So far we stated continuity for functions defined on an open interval I ‚äÜ R. In economic problems, the domain often has boundaries (e.g. x ‚Č• 0). It is natural to consider a closed interval [a, b].

14/18   Continuity on a Closed Interval

So far we stated continuity for functions defined on an open interval I ‚äÜ R. In economic problems, the domain often has boundaries (e.g. x ‚Č• 0). It is natural to consider a closed interval [a, b].

Definition 2

Let a < b and let f : [a, b] ‚Üí R. (a) f is continuous at xŐā ‚ąą [a, b] if for every ŌĶ > 0 there exists some őī > 0 such that for all x ‚ąą [a, b],

|x ‚ąí xŐā| < őī =‚áí |f (x) ‚ąí f (xŐā)| < ŌĶ.

(b) f is a continuous function if it is continuous at every point of its domain [a, b].

14/18   Endpoints and One-Sided Continuity

Let f : [a, b] ‚Üí R and consider continuity of f at xŐā. ‚ó¶ If xŐā ‚ąą (a, b), the idea is exactly the same as before: we want to get close to f (xŐā) by approaching xŐā.

‚ó¶ If xŐā = a, then x ‚ąą [a, b] forces x ‚Č• a, so we are only allowed to approach a from the right.

‚ó¶ If xŐā = b, then x ‚ąą [a, b] forces x ‚Č§ b, so we are only allowed to approach b from the left.

15/18   Intermediate Value Theorem

Proposition 3

Let f : [a, b] ‚Üí R be continuous. Take any number y ‚ąą R satisfying

min{f (a), f (b)} ‚Č§ y ‚Č§ max{f (a), f (b)}.

Then there exists some c ‚ąą [a, b] such that f (c) = y.

The IVT formalizes the idea that a continuous function f cannot ‚Äújump over‚ÄĚ any value in its range.

Key Ideas ‚ó¶ Domain: a closed interval [a, b] ‚ÄĒ no gaps in the domain. ‚ó¶ Function: f continuous on [a, b] ‚ÄĒ continuity ensures no gaps in the range as well.

a c b x

Continuity ensures the graph must cross the blue horizontal line somewhere between a and b.

17/18   Economic Application

The IVT is a simple existence tool for one-dimensional equilibrium problems.

Existence of Market Clearing Price ‚ó¶ Let pL ‚Č§ p ‚Č§ pH denote the price of a good.

‚ó¶ Let D(p) and S(p) be the demand and supply functions.

‚ó¶ The excess demand function is z(p) = D(p) ‚ąí S(p).

18/18   Economic Application

The IVT is a simple existence tool for one-dimensional equilibrium problems.

Existence of Market Clearing Price ‚ó¶ Let pL ‚Č§ p ‚Č§ pH denote the price of a good.

‚ó¶ Let D(p) and S(p) be the demand and supply functions.

‚ó¶ The excess demand function is z(p) = D(p) ‚ąí S(p).

‚ó¶ Reasonable assumptions: ‚ó¶ z(pL ) > 0 [shortage at lowest price]; ‚ó¶ z(pH ) < 0 [surplus at highest price].

18/18   Economic Application

The IVT is a simple existence tool for one-dimensional equilibrium problems.

Existence of Market Clearing Price ‚ó¶ Let pL ‚Č§ p ‚Č§ pH denote the price of a good.

‚ó¶ Let D(p) and S(p) be the demand and supply functions.

‚ó¶ The excess demand function is z(p) = D(p) ‚ąí S(p).

‚ó¶ Reasonable assumptions: ‚ó¶ z(pL ) > 0 [shortage at lowest price]; ‚ó¶ z(pH ) < 0 [surplus at highest price].

‚ó¶ If z is continuous,then there exists p‚ąó ‚ąą (pL , pH ) such that

z(p‚ąó ) = 0, or equivalently D(p‚ąó ) = S(p‚ąó ).
